title: Runtime Request Schemas, Negotiation, Problem Details, and OpenAPI · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs runtime request schemas, negotiation, problem details, and openapi from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for runtime-validated documented API boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.
Define string, number, boolean, date, enum, array, empty, repeated, and default coercion explicitly and test ambiguity.
Map stable domain and infrastructure causes to bounded RFC 9457 types, statuses, field evidence, correlation, and retry guidance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Request Unknown Admission · changed case · Review Feed Ts R…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 7118 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove runtime request admission, coercion, problem details, negotiation, and OpenAPI conformance; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.

stimulus.title: Feed Ts Openapi Conformance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 2536 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove runtime request admission, coercion, problem details, negotiation, and OpenAPI conformance; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate or compare OpenAPI 3.2 operations and schemas against runtime validators, auth, examples, errors, pagination, and compatibility tests.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A generated OpenAPI file cannot drift from actual middleware.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate or compare OpenAPI 3.2 operations and schemas against runtime validators, auth, examples, errors, pagination, and compatibility tests. Artifact under review: runtime-validated documented API boundary.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Openapi Conformance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate or compare OpenAPI 3.2 operations and schemas against runtime validators, auth, examples, errors, pagination, and compatibility tests.
